# Phase 6: Full-Video Run & Post-Processing - Context

**Gathered:** 2026-03-31

<domain>
## Phase Boundary

Run player_tracker.py on the full 71-minute standard-1920x1080-followcam.mp4, implement tracklet gap-merge post-processing (< 5s gap threshold), and validate player_ids.json covers the full video duration.

The full run will take approximately 6 hours CPU time. Write the code and start the run, then validate the output and implement gap-merge on whatever output exists (or on the 5-minute benchmark segment for validation purposes).

</domain>

<decisions>
## Implementation Decisions

### Locked Decisions (from spec)
- Tracklet gap-merge: < 5 seconds = merge (at 30fps = < 150 frames)
- player_ids.json: ~/veo-clips/player-continuity-tracking/player_ids.json
- Must complete in ≤ 8 hours wall-clock

### Architecture
- Gap-merge is a post-processing step on the annotation JSON
- Identify target player's track segments (frames where track_id matches target)
- Merge segments where gap < 5s between consecutive appearances
- Write merged output as player_ids.json (the final annotation file)

### Claude's Discretion
- Gap-merge algorithm: sort frames by frame_idx, find target player track_ids, merge consecutive segments

</decisions>
